subject: DevOps
title: Capital One Cloud Breach (2019)
description: A micro-lesson that makes Capital One Cloud Breach (2019) usable.

hook.text: A former AWS employee exploited a misconfigured firewall. 100 million records were stolen.

buildup.text: Capital One's WAF was misconfigured, allowing SSRF attacks against their cloud metadata.

discovery.text: The attacker accessed IAM credentials via the metadata service and read S3 buckets.

twist.text: The data had been in the cloud for years. The misconfiguration went undetected.

climax.text: AWS made IMDSv2 the default after this. Token-based metadata access is now standard.

punchline.text: Lock down cloud IAM. The shared responsibility model is real.

quiz.question: How did the attacker access Capital One's data?
options: Through a misconfigured firewall allowing SSRF to cloud metadata
By physically breaking into a data center
